description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a striking color scheme of blue, red, and white, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The fuselage is predominantly blue, with a rounded nose featuring red and white concentric stripes and a visible wooden propeller at the front. The biplane has two sets of straight, parallel wings, both painted in a deep red, supported by black struts. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all adorned with red, white, and blue striping. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a single seat visible. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable, suitable for grass or dirt runways.
description: A vintage blue biplane with red wings, a striped nose, and an open single-seat cockpit. It features fixed landing gear and a colorful tail with red, white, and blue stripes.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the striped nose, wooden propeller, and both red wings with supporting struts clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the blue fuselage, open cockpit, and the colorful tail section with its vertical and horizontal stabilizers.
description_view_2: Rear-right view focusing on the tail assembly, open cockpit, and the alignment of the red wings and black struts.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the propeller, striped nose, and the fixed landing gear with both main wheels visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings mounted one above the other, supported by vertical struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a fixed tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage.
